title: Piquant Mexican Monterey Jack Huaraches
description: A fermented, aerated brunch dish featuring shredded Monterey Jack cheese and fermented salsa, wrapped in dry‑brined corn tortillas. These piquant huaraches deliver heat and tang in every bite.
instructions: Dry brine the corn tortillas by sprinkling sea salt on each side and letting them rest at room temperature for 30 minutes to enhance flavor and texture.
Warm a skillet over medium heat, add olive oil, and lightly toast the brine‑brined tortillas until they puff slightly, about 1–2 minutes per side.
In a small bowl, mix shredded Monterey Jack cheese with ground cumin and smoked paprika to season the cheese.
Warm the shredded cheese in the skillet for 1 minute so it melts slightly, then remove from heat.
Spread a thin layer of fermented tomato salsa onto each toasted tortilla.
Spoon the warm, spiced cheese over the salsa, covering the tortilla evenly.
Top with fermented jalapeño slices for a kick of heat and a pop of fermented flavor.
Drizzle lime juice over the assembled huaraches and sprinkle chopped cilantro for freshness.
Serve immediately, optionally adding a side of sliced avocado for creaminess and extra nutrients.
Enjoy the piquant, fermented Mexican Monterey Jack huaraches as a brunch‑style dish.
